##NAMES=Juanitaite
##RRUFFID=R250064
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=(Cu,Ca,Fe)_10_Bi(AsO_4_)_4_(OH)_11_·2H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Gold Hill mine, Tooele County, Utah, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Olive-green to grass-green square crystal plates in sheaf-like subparallel aggregates associated with conichalcite and mixite on limonite. Raman data taken on a sample from the type find. Other data from the type sample, described in Kampf et al (2000)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250064
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Cu_7.03_Ca_2.39_Fe_0.50_)Bi_0.99_(AsO_4_)_4_(OH)_10.90_·2.22H_2_O
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 9.961 b: 9.961 c: 29.19 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 2896 crystal system: tetragonal
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
